steps.title: Find the radii
steps.content:
The outside diameter is $60$, so the outside radius is $30$. Subtract the wall thickness to get the inside radius.
$$
r=30-\frac{5}{8}=\frac{235}{8}
$$

steps.title: Set up wall volume
steps.content:
The wall volume is the outside cylinder volume minus the inside cylinder volume.
$$
V=\pi h(R^2-r^2)
$$
$$
V=132\pi(30^2-(\frac{235}{8})^2)
$$

steps.title: Compute the difference
steps.content:
Use difference of squares for the radius part.
$$
30^2-(\frac{235}{8})^2=(30-\frac{235}{8})(30+\frac{235}{8})
$$
$$
=\frac{5}{8}\cdot\frac{475}{8}=\frac{2{,}375}{64}
$$

steps.title: Choose the closest value
steps.content: Now multiply by the height and by $\pi$. $$ V=132\pi(\frac{2{,}375}{64}) $$ This is about $15{,}391$, so the closest listed value is $15{,}389$. The answer is D.
